one_time_description: Impairment of portfolio investments recorded as a non-recurring loss
economic_notes: COVID-19 impacted operations; First Nation closed to public March 2020, reopened August 2020. ISC approved unspent 2020-2021 funding to be utilized in 2021-2022. Consolidated entities include Seine River First Nation Development Corporation and Mudges Camp (2018) LTD. Portfolio investments include interests in Rainy Lake Tribal Development Corporation, Boundary Waters Anishinaabeg Development LP, Senic River Mall Inc., and Rainy River District First Nations Trust.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2021 not 2024. Province is Ontario (Thunder Bay area). Two subsidiaries fully consolidated: Seine River First Nation Development Corporation and Mudges Camp (2018) LTD. No schedule of remuneration provided. ISC funding of $3,360,514 recognized as revenue; total federal transfers $4,167,690 includes Health Canada ($364,652), CMHC ($216,431), FedNor ($226,093). Total government transfers = federal $4,167,690 + provincial $931,887 = $5,099,577. OSR includes Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership revenue, rental, other, trust fund, hydro settlement, recovery of funding, and gain on settlement. Impairment of portfolio investments $663,956 is a significant non-recurring item. No budget prepared for this year. COVID-19 note included.
